heading: What's New in 7.1
body:
The Node.js driver v7.1 release includes the following features:

- Adds logic for `MONGODB-AWS` authentication to the driver, so the
  `aws4` package is no longer required to use `MONGODB-AWS` authentication.
  To learn more about `MONGODB-AWS` authentication, see
  the [AWS IAM Authentication Mechanism](node-authentication-aws) guide.

- Improves runtime compatibility by eliminating all uses of `util.promisify`.

- Uses explicit `node:process` import instead of `global.process` so
  bundlers and alternate runtimes can supply and optimize the process
  implementation more consistently.

- Replaces several Node-specific APIs with standards-based equivalents to reduce
  the number of patches required to run the driver outside of Node.js:

  - `process.arch` is now `os.arch()`

  - `process.platform` is now `os.platform()`

  - `os.endianness()` is now `BSON.NumberUtils`

  - `process.hrtime()` is now `performance.now()`

  - `process.nextTick()` is now `queueMicrotask()`

- Improves retry behavior:

  - Prevents unnecessary connection pool churn when server-side connection rate
    limiting is enabled by no longer clearing the pool when connections fail.

  - Adds exponential backoff to `withTransaction()` retry behavior.

  - Deprioritizes overloaded or failed servers during server selection retries.

- OIDC authentication improvements:

  - Expands the default `OIDC ALLOWED_HOSTS` list to include `*.mongo.com`.

  - Fixes OIDC reauthentication when `promoteValues: false` is enabled.

- Corrects read preference handling for aggregations using `$merge` and `$out`.

- Deprecates `RenameCollectionOptions.new_collection` for future removal

To learn more about this release, see the
[v7.1.0 Release Notes](https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/releases/tag/v7.1.0) on
GitHub.
